flashcards.back: A simplified version of reality that helps us understand and predict. Not 100% accurate, but good enough to work.

flashcards.front: Why do physicists use models instead of studying reality exactly?
flashcards.back: Reality is too complicated. Models simplify it so we can calculate and understand what matters.

flashcards.front: Does a model have to be perfect?
flashcards.back: No. A model just needs to be accurate enough for the situation. Different situations need different models.

flashcards.front: What does it mean when a model 'breaks'?
flashcards.back: It stops being accurate. Usually happens when conditions change (temperature, speed, size). Time for a new model!
